body.title: Remote Igor bootstrap — launch Igor on a new box from swarm manager
body.description:
## Consolidated into T-swarm-manager 2026-04-19

Per Akien: remote-igor-bootstrap is not a separate ticket — it is a workflow of SwarmManager. See T-swarm-manager for the consolidated spec. Keeping this ticket open as a scope-flag: the "bootstrap a new Igor on a new box" work is the concrete deliverable once SwarmManager's scaffold lands. Close when T-swarm-manager ships the add_box flow.

## Original one-paragraph summary (still accurate)

GH-264 successor. SwarmManager can bootstrap a new Igor instance on a remote box: push env template, git pull, install deps, launch. Windows + Linux. Depends on SwarmManager + SystemProxy existing.

**Affected files:** lab/utility_closet/swarm_manager.py (the consolidated home — see T-swarm-manager); platform-specific install scripts under lab/utility_closet/installer/ (linux + windows). No new top-level files — this ticket's deliverable is a workflow on swarm_manager, not a standalone module.

**Design rules:** docs-in-code (the add_box workflow lives in swarm_manager; document there); no-secrets-in-repo (env template excludes creds).

**Scope boundary:** IN — add_box workflow on SwarmManager: push env template, git pull, install deps, launch on a new box. Linux + Windows. OUT — multi-datacenter federation; per-OS installer hardening beyond linux+windows.

**Test plan:** tests/test_swarm_manager_add_box.py — mocked SSH/transport asserts the right commands fire in the right order. E2E manual smoke against a fresh VM (linux first, windows second). Ticket closes when T-swarm-manager ships add_box.
